description: A U.S. service member was killed on Saturday “during a controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance” in northern Iraq, CENTCOM confirmed.

title: 2 US troops killed, 1 missing after Iranian missile, drone attack in Jordan
description: Two U.S. service members were killed in action on Friday in an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack on U.S. and partner forces in Jordan.

title: US Navy 2 years behind on hypersonic missile installation on Zumwalt destroyers
description: The U.S. Navy’s expensive effort to install hypersonic missiles on three Zumwalt-class destroyers is two years behind schedule.

title: Saronic picks Brownsville, Texas, for $3 billion Port Alpha shipyard
description: Autonomous vessel maker Saronic is set to build Port Alpha, a shipyard the company describes as the most advanced in the world, in Texas.

title: Canada to spend $1.4 billion on armored combat support vehicles built locally
description: Canada is significantly expanding its armored fleet with a new contract to acquire 190 combat support vehicles over the next four years.

title: Wars shift focus from jets to weapons at giant Farnborough Airshow
description: Spiraling security risks are expected to push defense to the forefront of Britain’s Farnborough Airshow next week.

title: 2 defense tech companies sue US Navy after losing out on MUSV program
description: Two defense tech companies sued the U.S. government, alleging they were unfairly excluded from the Navy's Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel program.

title: Russian troops survive just 20-30 minutes on Ukraine’s front lines, CIA director says
description: Russian recruits arriving on the battlefield in Ukraine survive an estimated 20 to 30 minutes before they are killed or wounded, the CIA director said.

title: Saab to supply sensors and combat systems for new German warships after F126 scrapped
description: Swedish defense firm Saab has secured an 8.7 billion kronor ($900 million) contract to supply combat systems and sensors for the German Navy’s MEKO ships.

title: Franco-German defense cooperation under strain as Macron, Merz meet
description: The meetings come weeks after industrial rivalries forced Paris and Berlin to scrap the landmark Future Combat Air System (FCAS) fighter jet program.

title: Fedorov out as Ukraine’s defense minister in major government shake-up
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismissed Mykhailo Fedorov as the country's minister of defense after just six months in office.

title: US Air Force turns to cheaper cruise missiles it can buy by the thousands
description: The U.S. Air Force is turning to cruise missiles it can buy by the thousand, cheap enough to shoot many for less than it once cost to shoot one.

title: MBDA appoints Airbus executive Jean-Brice Dumont as new CEO
description: MBDA, Europe’s largest missile maker, appointed Airbus Defence and Space executive Jean-Brice Dumont as its new chief executive officer effective Nov. 1.

title: First the coast guard, then an ICBM: China tests new, long-term ways to hold off rivals in Asia
description: Experts say Beijing used both methods to try out new, attention-grabbing and long-term ways to deter others from challenging its territorial claims.

title: Pressure mounts on US defense executives to boost weapons production
description: Defense executives face pressure to boost production as prolonged conflicts have consumed large quantities of missiles, interceptors and other weapons.

title: US launches new round of strikes against Iran
description: The U.S. conducted a new wave of strikes against Iranian military sites on Wednesday after reimposing a naval blockade of the Islamic Republic's ports.

title: Israeli firm wants to convert commercial ships into floating drone bases
description: Elbit Systems detailed a new maritime concept centered on its Hermes 650 Spark unmanned aircraft, envisioning the conversion of commercial surface ships.

title: European firms plan exo-atmospheric interceptor for 2027 test
description: Bliksem EXO is meant to defeat ballistic threats including Russia’s intermediate-range Oreshnik missile with separating and maneuvering re-entry vehicles.

title: The US Navy is eyeing next-gen carrier-based drones. Here’s what they might do.
description: The Navy wants its next-gen carrier-based drones to handle a wide range of missions, from strikes and anti-submarine warfare to aerial refueling and more.

title: Navy awards TOTE $2.2 billion to manage Medium Landing Ships project
description: The Navy awarded TOTE $2.2 billion to manage its medium landing ship construction efforts, with the first vessel scheduled for delivery in fall 2029.

title: Manufacturing woes hamper US 155-mm ammo production
description: Manufacturing problems are dashing the U.S. Army’s plans to boost production of urgently needed 155-mm howitzer shells, a government watchdog said.

title: Europe’s defense build-up is delivering for NATO — and America
description: "We invite our American friends to judge us not by old assumptions, but by the record we are building together," writes the EU top envoy to the U.S.

title: Ukraine agrees on plan to acquire 16 Rafale jets, France’s Macron says
description: Ukraine has agreed on a plan to acquire 16 Rafale fighter jets from France together with the accompanying weapon systems, along with air-defense equipment.

description: Ballistic missiles are the one threat Ukraine can't stop with a weapon it makes itself.

description: Lawmakers demanded Monday that the Pentagon release its findings from an investigation into the Feb. 28 strike at a girls’ school in Iran.

description: The acquisition, which involves a concurrent licensing deal with startup Mirador, gives Jasper a chance to rebound from setbacks that have wiped out most of its market value.

title: Merck wins first approval of a PCSK9 cholesterol pill

title: Celcuity gains FDA approval for closely watched breast cancer drug
description: Still, shares fell by nearly 20% after the company revealed a delay in the coming launch of Revtorpyk. Details in the drug’s prescribing information surprised some analysts, too.

title: Merck ADC, in China study, shows potential to replace chemo in lung cancer
description: The results were seen by Wall Street analysts as “direct proof-of-concept” that sac-TMT, a drug Merck views as a “cornerstone,” could be part of a future treatment standard for front-line lung cancer.

title: Braveheart, Attovia join next wave of biotech IPOs
description: The two startups are the third and fourth, respectively, to outline offerings this month and aim to capitalize on momentum that’s already yielded several big-ticket IPOs.
